payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the current memory for Sam from Goldman. It filtered mem_old because mem_old is superseded by mem_new, so presenting mem_old as current would violate the rule to avoid surfacing superseded facts as active memories.
payload.decision: mem_new was returned because it is the active, non-superseded memory matching the query. mem_old was filtered because it has been superseded by mem_new and should not be presented as current.

entity_resolution.reason: The query context identifies Sam with Goldman; no durable entity ID was provided in the fixture, so the explanation preserves the provided labels without inventing an ID.

payload.intent: Explain a recall/debug decision for Sam from Goldman, specifically why mem_new was returned and mem_old was filtered.

memory_cards.statement: For the entity Sam associated with Goldman, recall selected mem_new as the current memory and excluded mem_old because mem_old is marked superseded.

details: Explained recall behavior for fixture debug_explain_recall_sam.
Preserved supersession status: mem_new is current; mem_old is not current.

repair_options: If mem_old should still be visible, inspect whether it was incorrectly marked as superseded.
If mem_new is incorrect, update or retract mem_new before allowing mem_old to become current again.
If both memories contain distinct non-conflicting facts, split the supersession link so only the outdated portion is filtered.
